heading: Lab: :: Hospital Course:__page_no__4
text: lab: hospital course: 96 year old male with past medical history of hypothyroidism, osteoarthritis of knee status post placement presented to the er status post fall. patient endorses lower back pain that is chronic but worsening in nature and radiating down both his feet. he reports heaviness and burning in his feet. he denies any loss of sensation. he denies any loss of bowel control. he reports having history of arthritis in his back and previously had sessions of physical therapy which did not help him too much. in the er patient was hemodynamically stable. ct lumbar spine showed severe spinal stenosis at l4-l5 and foraminal narrowing at l5-s1. patient admitted for mri and physical therapy assessment. patient was evaluated by neurosurgery in the er. mri showed moderate to severe lumbar stenosis. patient's pain largely resolved. neurosurgery signed off. recommended physical therapy and epidural injection if needed. patient discharged home with home pt.

heading: HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:__page_no__8
text: history of present illness: 96 year old male with past medical history of hypothyroidism, osteoarthritis of knee status post placement presented to the er status post fall. patient endorses lower back pain that is chronic but worsening in nature and radiating down both his feet. he reports heaviness and burning in his feet. he denies any loss of sensation. he denies any loss of bowel control. he reports having history of arthritis in his back and previously had sessions of physical therapy which did not help him too much. in the er patient was hemodynamically stable. ct lumbar spine showed severe spinal stenosis at l4-l5 and foraminal narrowing at l5-s1. patient admitted for mri and physical therapy assessment. patient was evaluated by neurosurgery in the er.
